status.state_description: Waiting for human approval of the plan
status.product_description: Enterprise internal communications platform with channels, threads, file sharing, video conferencing, employee directory, and IT admin controls.

plan.description: Define business requirements, user personas, and process flows for the internal communications platform covering channels, threads, file sharing, video, directory, and admin controls.
acceptance_criteria: BRD approved by at least 2 stakeholders
User personas defined for: end user, IT admin, HR manager
Process flows cover all 6 feature areas
ROI model shows payback period with assumptions documented
Integration points with existing systems mapped
Migration plan drafted

plan.description: Produce product requirements document (PRD), feature roadmap, prioritization matrix, and success metrics for the internal_comms platform.
acceptance_criteria: PRD covers all 6 feature areas with acceptance criteria per feature
Roadmap defines MVP vs post-MVP scope
Success metrics include DAU, message latency P95, uptime SLA, and adoption rate
Prioritization matrix signed off by product and engineering leads

plan.description: Draft terms of service, privacy policy, data retention policy, and IP/licensing review for the internal communications platform.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy policy covers data collected, retention periods, and employee rights
Terms of service reviewed by legal counsel
OSS license inventory completed with no copyleft conflicts
Data retention policy defines TTLs for messages, files, and audit logs

plan.description: Create compliance framework mapping platform controls to ISO 27001 Annex A and SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria. Produce initial risk matrix and control evidence templates.
acceptance_criteria: Every ISO 27001 Annex A control has an owner and implementation status
SOC 2 CC1-CC9 criteria mapped to platform features
Risk register contains likelihood, impact, and mitigation per risk
Evidence collection plan defines artifact type, owner, and cadence per control

plan.description: Conduct user research and produce wireframes and interaction designs for channels, threads, file sharing, video conferencing, employee directory, and IT admin console.
acceptance_criteria: Wireframes cover all 6 feature areas and mobile-responsive layouts
User research conducted with minimum 5 representative users
Accessibility audit shows WCAG 2.1 AA compliance plan
Interaction design reviewed and approved by product manager

plan.description: Produce threat model, security architecture design, and penetration test plan for the internal communications platform. Define encryption-at-rest and in-transit requirements.
acceptance_criteria: STRIDE threat model covers all external-facing surfaces
Security architecture defines auth, authz, encryption, and secrets management
Pentest plan covers OWASP Top 10, API security, and privilege escalation
SBOM generated and no critical CVEs in direct dependencies

plan.description: Design OpenAPI 3.1 specifications for all platform APIs: messaging, channels, threads, file management, video signaling, employee directory, and IT admin endpoints.

payload.auth_scheme: Bearer JWT + API key for service-to-service

acceptance_criteria: All endpoints have request/response schemas with examples
Error responses follow RFC 7807 Problem Details format
All APIs pass OpenAPI linting with zero errors
Auth requirements documented per endpoint

plan.description: Design and implement database schema for users, channels, messages, threads, attachments, reactions, employee directory, and audit logs. Include migrations and seed data.
acceptance_criteria: Schema supports soft deletes and message edit history
All foreign keys and indexes documented
Migrations are idempotent and reversible
Audit_events table captures actor, action, resource, and timestamp for SOC 2

plan.description: Set up the agentic AI pattern (multi_agent_coordinator) configuration for the internal_comms platform. Configure coordinator agent for smart notifications, content moderation suggestions, and meeting summarization.

components.notification_agent: intelligent notification batching and priority scoring
components.moderation_agent: flags policy-violating content for IT admin review

acceptance_criteria: project.yaml defines all active modules and agent roles
prompts.yaml contains system prompts for coordinator, notification, moderation, and summary agents
tasks.yaml registers task types: summarize_thread, flag_content, batch_notifications, summarize_meeting
Config validated by SAGE framework loader without errors

plan.description: Build the core messaging backend: real-time WebSocket server, channel management, threaded messaging, message persistence, reactions, and @mentions with notification fanout.
acceptance_criteria: Message delivery latency P95 < 200ms under 1000 concurrent users
WebSocket reconnects automatically with message catch-up on disconnect
Edit and delete history retained for audit compliance
Mentions trigger push notification within 1 second

plan.description: Build file sharing service: upload, download, virus scanning, preview generation, access control, storage quota enforcement, and file search.

payload.storage: S3-compatible object storage (MinIO for on-prem, S3 for cloud)

acceptance_criteria: Files encrypted at rest with AES-256 and served over TLS 1.3
Virus scan runs before file is marked available (ClamAV integration)
Upload progress reported via SSE
File access restricted to channel members

plan.description: Build employee directory service: profile management, org chart hierarchy, search, SCIM 2.0 provisioning from IdP, and presence/status indicators.
acceptance_criteria: SCIM 2.0 endpoint passes Azure AD and Okta provisioning tests
Org chart API returns hierarchy up to 10 levels deep
Directory search returns results in < 100ms for 10,000 employee orgs
Presence status updates propagate to all connected clients within 2 seconds

plan.description: Build IT admin controls backend: user provisioning/deprovisioning, role-based access control (RBAC), data retention policy enforcement, audit log export, content policy management, and SSO configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Deprovisioning revokes all sessions and channel access within 60 seconds
RBAC supports custom roles with permission inheritance
Audit log export covers 90-day window with filtering by actor/action/resource
Retention policy automatically purges messages and files per configured TTL

plan.description: Build video conferencing signaling server using WebRTC: room management, SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit) integration, recording, and calendar integration hooks.
acceptance_criteria: Video call latency < 150ms under normal network conditions
Supports up to 50 simultaneous video feeds in a room
Recording saved to object storage with access restricted to room organizer and IT admin
Room creation API returns join link usable without account for guests (configurable)

plan.description: Build the main messaging UI: channel sidebar, message feed, threaded replies panel, rich text composer with attachments, emoji reactions, and search interface.
acceptance_criteria: Message feed renders 1000 messages with virtual scrolling without frame drops
File uploads show progress bar and preview on completion
Search results highlight matched terms and filter by channel, date, and author
UI matches approved wireframes at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px)

plan.description: Build employee directory UI: searchable org chart, profile pages, presence indicators, and directory admin panel for custom field management.
acceptance_criteria: Org chart renders up to 500 nodes with pan/zoom
Profile page loads in < 500ms including avatar
Presence status updates live without page refresh
Directory search debounced at 300ms, results appear within 200ms

plan.description: Build video conferencing UI: in-call layout with grid/speaker view, screen share, chat sidebar, participant controls, and pre-call device check.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-call device check validates camera, mic, and speaker with live preview
Layout switches between grid and speaker view without call interruption
Screen share tile renders at full available resolution
Recording indicator visible to all participants within 2 seconds of recording start

plan.description: Build IT admin console UI: user management table, RBAC editor, audit log viewer with export, retention policy configurator, SSO/SCIM setup wizard, and content policy editor.
acceptance_criteria: User management table supports bulk actions (suspend, deprovision, role change)
Audit log viewer handles 1M+ records with server-side pagination and filtering
SSO setup wizard validates SAML metadata before saving
Retention policy UI shows countdown to next execution

plan.description: Write unit and integration tests for all backend services: messaging, file sharing, directory, admin controls, and video signaling.
acceptance_criteria: Overall code coverage >= 80% across all backend services
All API endpoints have at least one happy-path and one error-path test
Integration tests run against real Postgres and Redis (no mocks for persistence layer)
Tests pass in CI in under 5 minutes

plan.description: Develop QA test plan, test case suite, and exploratory testing charters covering all 6 feature areas. Include regression, usability, and security acceptance tests.
acceptance_criteria: Test plan reviewed and approved by product manager
Test cases cover all PRD acceptance criteria
Security acceptance tests include MFA bypass attempt, privilege escalation, and file access control
Exploratory charters defined for each feature area with session time-boxes

plan.description: Set up infrastructure as code for the internal_comms platform: Kubernetes manifests, Helm charts, PostgreSQL HA cluster, Redis cluster, Elasticsearch, MinIO, and mediasoup node pool.
acceptance_criteria: All infra is reproducible from code with a single `terraform apply`
PostgreSQL HA with automatic failover < 30 seconds
Secrets stored in Vault or K8s Secrets with RBAC access controls
Staging environment mirrors production topology

plan.description: Set up CI/CD pipelines, monitoring (Prometheus + Grafana), alerting, log aggregation, and SRE runbooks for the internal_comms platform.
acceptance_criteria: CI pipeline runs lint, tests, and security scan on every PR
CD pipeline deploys to staging automatically on main merge, production requires manual approval
SLO dashboards display error budget burn rate with 7-day and 30-day windows
Alerting fires within 2 minutes of SLO breach

plan.description: Execute system-level end-to-end tests covering cross-service flows: user onboarding via SCIM → channel join → message send with attachment → video call → admin audit log verification.
acceptance_criteria: All critical user journeys pass E2E without manual intervention
Load test at 5000 concurrent users keeps message latency P95 < 200ms
Video conferencing load test with 50 active rooms (20 participants each) shows CPU < 80%
Audit log captures every action in the E2E run with correct actor attribution
Load test simulates expected concurrent users
Failover scenario tested

plan.description: Define SLA agreements, operational runbooks for incident response, on-call rotation, capacity planning model, and disaster recovery procedures.
acceptance_criteria: SLA document defines uptime targets, measurement methodology, and credit schedule
Incident response runbook covers P1-P4 severity with escalation paths and communication templates
Disaster recovery runbook tested with a tabletop exercise
Capacity planning model forecasts storage and compute for 12 months at 3 growth scenarios

plan.description: Collect and organize evidence artifacts for ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II readiness: access control logs, encryption certificates, vulnerability scan reports, and change management records.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence mapped to each ISO 27001 Annex A control and SOC 2 criterion
No critical gaps in evidence for CC6 (Logical Access) or CC7 (System Operations)
Readiness assessment scores >= 80% for both frameworks
Gap remediation plan created for any control below threshold

plan.description: Produce end-user onboarding guide, IT admin configuration guide, video tutorial scripts, and quick-reference cards for the internal_comms platform.
acceptance_criteria: User guide covers account setup, channel navigation, messaging, file sharing, and video calls
Admin guide covers SSO setup, user provisioning, RBAC, audit log export, and retention policies
Video scripts for top 5 user journeys reviewed and approved by UX designer
Quick reference card fits on one A4 page and covers daily use cases
Admin guide separate from end-user guide
Role-based training paths defined

plan.description: Write developer API documentation, architecture decision records (ADRs), deployment runbook, and internal engineering wiki pages.
acceptance_criteria: API docs auto-generated from OpenAPI specs and hosted at /docs
At least 4 ADRs covering major architectural decisions with alternatives considered
Deployment runbook validated by a junior engineer doing a dry-run deployment
Architecture diagram reviewed by tech lead

flaws: Step 10 leaves the backend stack as 'Python FastAPI + WebSockets / Node.js + Socket.IO' — an unresolved either/or. Mixing two runtimes for the same service layer creates split codebases, dual dependency trees, inconsistent error handling, and doubles the CI complexity. This must be decided before a line is written.

Step 14 has no TURN/STUN server design. WebRTC in enterprise environments fails silently for users behind symmetric NAT (common on corporate networks). Without coturn or a managed TURN service deployed in-region, video calls will not work for a material subset of enterprise users. This is not optional infrastructure.

The plan targets SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 (Steps 4, 25) but has no end-to-end encryption for messages. The threat model (Step 6) must explicitly evaluate E2E encryption and produce a signed-off decision record. Absence of that record means the compliance auditor will flag it.

GDPR right-to-erasure (Step 3 notes 'GDPR consideration') directly conflicts with the immutable audit log requirement for SOC 2/ISO 27001. Deleting a user must pseudonymize actor fields in audit records, cascade across PostgreSQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, and object storage, and coordinate with the compliance record. This contradiction is not designed around anywhere in the plan.

Step 14 sets 'max_participants_per_room: 200' but acceptance criteria only validates 50 simultaneous video feeds. At 50 rooms × 20 participants (Step 23 load target), that is 1000 concurrent video participants. The mediasoup node pool sizing and autoscaling policy are absent — this will underspecify the compute budget by an order of magnitude.
Step 19 tests messaging, files, directory, admin, and video signaling, but Step 14 (video) is not in its dependency list. Video signaling has no unit or integration test coverage in the plan.

Step 23 (system/load tests) depends on Steps 15-20 but not on Step 21 (infrastructure) or Step 22 (CI/CD). Load testing 5000 concurrent users requires production-topology infrastructure to be up. Running load tests against a developer laptop is not a valid acceptance gate.

The Elasticsearch indexing pipeline from PostgreSQL at 1000 messages/second (Step 23 load target) is not designed. Without a CDC pipeline (Debezium or equivalent), writes to Postgres and writes to Elasticsearch will drift under load, producing stale or missing search results. This is a known production failure mode.

No mobile applications are in scope. Enterprise internal comms platforms (Slack, Teams, Webex) derive majority adoption from iOS and Android native apps. A responsive web app is not a substitute for push notifications via APNs/FCM, background sync, or camera/microphone access on mobile. This is a business-level gap that should be in the PRD or explicitly out-of-scope.

Step 11 uses ClamAV for virus scanning of files up to 500MB. ClamAV scans synchronously and can take 30-120 seconds on a large file. The acceptance criteria says 'virus scan runs before file is marked available' with no timeout or async queue design. Under concurrent uploads, this blocks the file service completely.

Virtual backgrounds (Step 14) require WebAssembly/TensorFlow.js ML segmentation models (tens of MB per client) or server-side processing. This is not a trivial checkbox — it needs a dedicated design decision on client-vs-server processing, model size budgets, and CPU impact during calls.

Step 9 injects SAGE framework YAML configuration (project.yaml, prompts.yaml, tasks.yaml) into the product build critical path at dependency position [2]. This is a meta-framework concern, not a product delivery milestone. Blocking backend work (Step 10 depends on 7+8; Step 9 depends on 2 independently) is fine structurally, but the acceptance criteria reference SAGE framework internals, not product outcomes.

Rate limiting is absent from the API design (Step 7), backend implementation (Steps 10-14), and security architecture (Step 6). A messaging platform without per-user/per-channel rate limits is trivially abusable for spam flooding and DoS within the tenant.
suggestions: Decide Python FastAPI vs Node.js in Step 2 (PRD) or add an explicit Architecture Decision Record step before Step 7. The ADRs in Step 27 are too late — they document decisions already made, not inform them.
Add a dedicated WebRTC infrastructure step (TURN/STUN cluster with coturn or Cloudflare Calls/Twilio) as a dependency for Step 14 and Step 23. Place it alongside Step 21.

Add a GDPR compliance design task after Step 3 that produces a 'User Data Deletion Runbook' — specifying which fields get pseudonymized in audit logs vs hard-deleted in transactional tables, with code-level implementation in Step 13.

Split Step 14 into two tasks: (a) WebRTC signaling server + room management, and (b) SFU capacity design + TURN infrastructure. Virtual backgrounds should be explicitly MVP-excluded or given its own task with ML model selection and client perf benchmarks.
Add a CDC/search indexing pipeline task (Debezium → Kafka → Elasticsearch consumer) between Steps 8 and 10. Define index mapping, update cadence, and lag SLO in the acceptance criteria.
Move Step 9 (SAGE config) to after Step 27 or make it a parallel non-blocking task. It should never block infrastructure (Step 21) or backend implementation.
Add a push notification service design to Step 10 — specify the broker (Firebase FCM, APNs for future mobile, web push via VAPID) and the notification fanout architecture explicitly.

Step 6 assigns STRIDE threat modeling and pentest planning to 'regulatory_specialist'. This work requires a security engineer or AppSec architect. Reassign the role and add a penetration test execution step (not just a plan) before Step 25.

Add key management design to Step 6: specify KMS (AWS KMS, HashiCorp Vault Transit, or GCP Cloud KMS), key rotation cadence, per-tenant key isolation strategy, and which services hold keys vs envelopes.

For Step 11 (virus scanning), define async scan queue architecture: file uploaded → marked 'pending_scan' → scan worker pulls from queue → marks available/quarantined. Set a scan timeout SLO and quarantine behavior.
missing: TURN/STUN server provisioning and geographic placement strategy — without this, WebRTC video fails in enterprise NAT environments.
Mobile application scope decision — iOS and Android are absent. Even a 'mobile web PWA' decision needs to be made explicit in the PRD.
CDC pipeline design for Postgres → Elasticsearch real-time indexing at scale.
Encryption key management architecture (KMS selection, rotation policy, envelope encryption per tenant).
Data migration tooling for organizations migrating from Slack, Teams, or existing tools — Step 1 mentions a migration plan but there is no implementation step.
Video recording consent notification — recording to S3 without explicit consent flow violates GDPR in EU jurisdictions and several US state laws.
Rate limiting specification (per-user, per-channel, per-IP) in both API design and backend implementation.

Database partitioning and archival strategy for the messages table — at 1000 msg/sec sustained, a flat PostgreSQL table will hit query performance walls within months without partition-by-date design.
Pentest execution (not just plan) — Step 6 produces a pentest plan but no step executes it and feeds findings back into Step 25 evidence.
Staging environment smoke test gate — there is no step between Step 21 (infra) and Step 23 (load test) that validates staging is functional before expensive load tests run.
security_risks: No TURN credential rotation mechanism. Static TURN credentials embedded in WebRTC offers can be harvested by any authenticated user and used to relay arbitrary traffic through your TURN servers — a bandwidth-abuse vector. Use time-limited TURN credentials (RFC 8489 Section 9.2).

File access control relies on 'per-channel access control' (Step 11) but the S3/MinIO presigned URL pattern is not specified. If presigned URLs are generated with long TTLs (common default is 7 days), a deprovisioned user retains file access until URL expiry — bypassing the 60-second deprovisioning SLA in Step 13.

SSO/SAML assertion replay is not addressed in the threat model scope. SAML 2.0 requires assertion ID tracking to prevent replay attacks. This is commonly missed in SAML implementations and must be in the pentest scope.

Elasticsearch is listed as storing message content for search. If Elasticsearch cluster is compromised, all message history is exposed in plaintext. Elasticsearch encryption at rest and field-level encryption for message bodies must be in Step 6.

The moderation agent (Step 9) reads message content to flag policy violations — this creates a trust boundary where an AI agent has access to all private channel messages. The access control model for this agent (service account scope, audit logging of agent reads) is not designed.

Step 13 'retention_policy_executor' automatically purges messages. If an attacker can manipulate retention policy configuration (via RBAC misconfiguration), they could trigger premature deletion of evidence during a security incident. The retention executor needs a 'legal hold' override that locks records against policy-based deletion.

Guest access for video rooms ('join link usable without account for guests — configurable', Step 14) creates an unauthenticated surface. Guest join links must be scoped to single-use or time-limited tokens, not permanent room IDs. This is not specified and defaults in many WebRTC implementations are insecure.
final_review.summary: This is an ambitious and structurally sound plan that covers the right domains — compliance, security, UX, backend, frontend, infra, and ops — with reasonable dependency ordering and specific acceptance criteria. However, it has two production-breaking gaps that alone justify the score: the complete absence of TURN/STUN infrastructure (video will fail for a significant fraction of enterprise users behind corporate NAT) and the unresolved GDPR-vs-audit-log immutability contradiction (which will surface as a compliance finding within the first audit cycle). The backend stack ambiguity (FastAPI vs Socket.IO) must be resolved before implementation begins or you get two codebases and double the maintenance cost. The search indexing pipeline gap means message search will produce stale results under production load. The plan also systematically underestimates WebRTC complexity — virtual backgrounds, 200-participant rooms, and 50-room load targets are each non-trivial engineering efforts treated as line items. At 58/100, the plan is a solid foundation that needs approximately 4-6 additional tasks (TURN infra, GDPR deletion design, CDC pipeline, pentest execution, mobile scope decision, KMS design) and the stack decision resolved before it is safe to begin implementation.

history.summary: This is an ambitious and structurally sound plan that covers the right domains — compliance, security, UX, backend, frontend, infra, and ops — with reasonable dependency ordering and specific acceptance criteria. However, it has two production-breaking gaps that alone justify the score: the complete absence of TURN/STUN infrastructure (video will fail for a significant fraction of enterprise users behind corporate NAT) and the unresolved GDPR-vs-audit-log immutability contradiction (which will surface as a compliance finding within the first audit cycle). The backend stack ambiguity (FastAPI vs Socket.IO) must be resolved before implementation begins or you get two codebases and double the maintenance cost. The search indexing pipeline gap means message search will produce stale results under production load. The plan also systematically underestimates WebRTC complexity — virtual backgrounds, 200-participant rooms, and 50-room load targets are each non-trivial engineering efforts treated as line items. At 58/100, the plan is a solid foundation that needs approximately 4-6 additional tasks (TURN infra, GDPR deletion design, CDC pipeline, pentest execution, mobile scope decision, KMS design) and the stack decision resolved before it is safe to begin implementation.
